message: agent1: working on 'Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion by Properly Closing File Contexts' - will modify src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and possibly src/click/core.py (make_context/Context.__exit__). Will keep changes scoped to context cleanup only.

agent2: implementing max_resources param on Context (src/click/core.py: __init__, with_resource, and src/click/shell_completion.py for resource_manager compatibility). Will touch Context.__init__ and with_resource primarily. Let me know if you're touching those.

agent2 update: I've added max_resources param to Context.__init__ (src/click/core.py, near _close_callbacks init) and updated with_resource() (core.py) to enforce/count the shared limit. In shell_completion.py I added 2 lines right at the top of _resolve_context (before 'ctx = cli.make_context(...)') that force ctx_args['resilient_parsing']=True and ctx_args['max_resources']=None so completion contexts never hit the resource limit. If you're touching _resolve_context or make_context/__exit__ for cleanup, please check for overlap with my max_resources lines - happy to rebase/merge my snippet around your cleanup logic. Let me know what you land on.

agent1: I'm about to rewrite _resolve_context in shell_completion.py entirely to wrap all 3 make_context() calls in 'with ... as ctx:' blocks (matches upstream click 8.2/8.3 fix) for proper resource cleanup during shell completion. The 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True' line stays as the first line of the function body, unchanged in position. In core.py I'm ONLY touching the docstring of Context.exit() (versionchanged 8.2 note) - no functional/init changes on my end, so no overlap with your max_resources work in __init__/with_resource. Please add your 'ctx_args["max_resources"] = None' line right after the existing resilient_parsing line once I push my edit - I'll ping you when done.

agent1: done. shell_completion.py's _resolve_context is now fully rewritten with 'with ... as ctx:' blocks around all 3 make_context() calls (function body now indented one level deeper, wrapped in the outer with). core.py only has a docstring change on Context.exit() - no functional changes to __init__/with_resource on my end, so it's safe for you to add max_resources there. One heads up: the first line of the function body is still 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True' followed immediately by 'with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:' - insert your 'ctx_args["max_resources"] = None' line between those two, before the with-statement consumes ctx_args. Patch written to patch.txt already; let me know if you need me to hold off on submitting.
